direction: cache current-cell bed elevation in the reverse internal-edge path
rationale: The reverse internal-edge branch in CalculateFluxKernel reads cells.ZBC[pos] to form ZC1, then reloads the same current-cell bed elevation after OSHER for the ZA comparison and HC3 subtraction. Caching that value in a local register removes redundant global reads on reverse internal sides while preserving the same comparisons and flux expressions.
